source: Census TIGERweb cartographic boundaries (place/county, simplified) + hand-encoded municipal foraging rules (June 2026)

jurisdictions.name: Dane County Parks
jurisdictions.label: Allowed
jurisdictions.limit: Edible fruits, nuts, and mushrooms may be foraged for personal (non-commercial) use; follow posted guidelines (leave enough for wildlife, no digging).
jurisdictions.note: Dane County Parks permits personal-use foraging system-wide. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance/policy.

jurisdictions.name: Ramsey County Parks
jurisdictions.label: Allowed
jurisdictions.limit: Ramsey County's 2022 Parks and Recreation Ordinance allows foraging fruits, nuts, and mushrooms on park property for personal use. Removing, altering, or damaging any other plant is not allowed, and digging or excavating is prohibited. Foraging is personal-use only; commercial or business use needs a permit.
jurisdictions.note: Ramsey County Parks permits personal-use foraging system-wide. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance/policy.
jurisdictions.sourceLabel: Ramsey County Parks and Recreation Ordinance (2022), Sec. 4

jurisdictions.name: Dakota County Parks
jurisdictions.label: Permit required
jurisdictions.limit: Under Dakota County Ordinance 107, foraging is allowed only for identified above-ground items in designated areas, with no digging or excavating. A Special Use Permit is required to forage any item, or in any area, not listed in the Park Rules and Regulations.
jurisdictions.note: Dakota County Parks's park ordinance allows removal only by permit. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Washington County Parks
jurisdictions.label: Permit required
jurisdictions.limit: Personal-use foraging of fungi, berries, nuts, seeds, flowers, and leaves (no roots) in designated areas is allowed with a free Foraging Permit.
jurisdictions.note: Washington County Parks's park ordinance allows removal only by permit. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Mecklenburg County Park & Recreation
jurisdictions.label: Prohibited
jurisdictions.limit: Mecklenburg County Park and Recreation lists collection, removal, or disturbance of any natural or cultural object as a prohibited activity, alongside damage or removal of park property. There is no personal-use foraging exception, so gathering plants, fruits, nuts, or fungi is not allowed in county parks and greenways.
jurisdictions.note: Mecklenburg County Park & Recreation's park ordinance allows removal only by permit. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Fairfax County Park Authority
jurisdictions.label: Permit required
jurisdictions.limit: Collecting plants or plant parts in Fairfax County Park Authority parks requires a permit from the managing agency; there is no general personal-use foraging exception.
jurisdictions.note: Fairfax County Park Authority's park ordinance allows removal only by permit. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.
jurisdictions.sourceLabel: Fairfax County Park Authority park rules

jurisdictions.name: Maricopa County Parks
jurisdictions.label: Prohibited
jurisdictions.limit: Removing, cutting, or injuring plants, fruit, or vegetation in Maricopa County Parks parks is prohibited without authorization.
jurisdictions.note: Maricopa County Parks's park ordinance protects all park vegetation; foraging is not permitted. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: San Diego
jurisdictions.label: Prohibited
jurisdictions.limit: San Diego Municipal Code section 63.0102(c)(4) makes it unlawful to injure, destroy, cut, or remove any tree or other natural vegetation in any park without the written permission of the City Manager. Foraging in San Diego city parks and beaches is not permitted.
jurisdictions.note: San Diego's park ordinance protects all park vegetation; foraging is not permitted. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Sacramento
jurisdictions.label: Prohibited
jurisdictions.limit: Sacramento City Code section 12.72.060 (Park use regulations) makes it a violation to intentionally remove, break, injure, deface, or disturb any plant material in a city park. Foraging or removing vegetation from Sacramento city parks is not permitted.
jurisdictions.note: Sacramento's park ordinance protects all park vegetation; foraging is not permitted. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Oakland
jurisdictions.label: Prohibited
jurisdictions.limit: Oakland Municipal Code section 12.64.090 makes it unlawful to pick flowers in any city park, or to cut, break, or in any way injure, damage, or deface the trees, shrubs, or turf. Collecting plants in Oakland parks is not allowed without park authorization. Occurrence data is not harvest permission.
jurisdictions.note: Oakland's park ordinance protects all park vegetation; foraging is not permitted. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: San Francisco
jurisdictions.label: Permit required
jurisdictions.limit: San Francisco Park Code section 4.06 bars removing or taking away any tree, shrub, flower, plant, grass, soil, or rock from any park without permission of the Recreation and Park Department. There is no general personal-use foraging exception, so collecting requires agency permission. Occurrence data is not harvest permission.
jurisdictions.note: San Francisco's park ordinance allows removal only by permit. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Oklahoma City
jurisdictions.label: Unverified
jurisdictions.limit: Oklahoma City Municipal Code chapter 38, which governs parks and recreation, contains no rule on collecting, picking, or removing plants and no foraging permit system; it centers on boating, reservoirs, and fishing. A permit requirement could not be confirmed, so treat this as Unverified. Occurrence data is not harvest permission.
jurisdictions.note: Oklahoma City's park ordinance allows removal only by permit. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Cleveland
jurisdictions.label: Prohibited
jurisdictions.limit: Cleveland Codified Ordinances section 623.17 makes it a criminal offense to remove, destroy, break, injure, deface, or otherwise tamper with plant material or other property in city parks, parkways, and recreational facilities. Foraging in Cleveland parks is prohibited. Occurrence data is not harvest permission.
jurisdictions.note: Cleveland's park ordinance protects all park vegetation; foraging is not permitted. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Cincinnati
jurisdictions.label: Prohibited
jurisdictions.limit: Foraging is prohibited in Cincinnati parks. Park Board Rules bar taking, removing, digging, cutting, or damaging any vegetation, living or dead, on park property. No general personal-use or permit exception for gathering is offered. Occurrence on the map is not permission. Gather instead on lands that explicitly allow it.
jurisdictions.note: Cincinnati's park ordinance allows removal only by permit. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Detroit
jurisdictions.label: Prohibited
jurisdictions.limit: Removing plants in Detroit parks is prohibited. The City of Detroit Park Rules, rule 18, direct visitors not to cut or deface trees, pick flowers, cut branches, harm park landscapes, remove plants, or dig in soils, under Ordinance 57-2-3. There is no personal-use foraging exception. Occurrence on the map is not permission.
jurisdictions.note: Detroit's park ordinance protects all park vegetation; foraging is not permitted. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.label: Prohibited

jurisdictions.note: Raleigh's park ordinance protects all park vegetation; foraging is not permitted. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Charlotte
jurisdictions.label: Prohibited
jurisdictions.limit: Removing plants in Charlotte parks is prohibited. City Code Section 15-132 bars damaging, cutting, transplanting, or removing any tree or plant, picking flowers or seeds, and digging or removing plants and soil. Mecklenburg County Park and Recreation rules likewise ban collecting or removing any natural object. No foraging permit pathway. Occurrence on the map is not permission.
jurisdictions.note: Charlotte's park ordinance allows removal only by permit. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Atlanta
jurisdictions.label: Permit required
jurisdictions.limit: Gathering in Atlanta parks needs written permission from the Parks Commissioner. Code Section 110-59 prohibits knowingly or recklessly destroying or damaging park plants except by that written permission, and it bars climbing trees or shrubs. No code section grants a general foraging allowance. Occurrence on the map is not permission.
jurisdictions.note: Atlanta's park ordinance allows removal only by permit. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.

jurisdictions.name: Omaha
jurisdictions.label: Prohibited
jurisdictions.limit: Omaha Municipal Code section 20-159 makes it unlawful to cut or injure any tree or shrub growing in a public square or park. Harvesting from park trees and shrubs is prohibited. The parks chapter sets no personal-use foraging permit. Occurrence data is not harvest permission.
jurisdictions.note: Omaha's park ordinance allows removal only by permit. Verified June 2026; see the linked ordinance.
